characters.name: Andrew Vida
characters.description: Andrew Vidais a recurring character in thefirst seasonofDamages. Andrew Vida became an employee atHewes & Associates. AfterPatty Hewesfinished arguing her case againstArthur Frobisheron a television interview with Greta Van Susteren, Andrew walked down the street with Patty, informing her thatTom Shayeshad confirmed a meeting with the client representatives for the following day. He also told her that he and his girlfriend were fans of Greta's and that he wished his girlfriend could have been there with them. When Patty expressed surprise that he had a girlfriend, he informed her that she was a teacher at the school her son,Michael Hewes, attended. Patty remarked that she thought Andrew was gay.

characters.name: Arthur Frobisher
characters.description: Arthur Frobisheris a main character in thefirst,second, andthirdseasons ofDamages. Frobisher's great-great-grandfather designed guns and became wealthy during the Civil War due to munitions demands. The Depression wiped out their wealth, except for his gun collection. He founded a private club, which remained standing over 100 years later and contained a display of his gun collection. Frobisher grew up with nothing and left his father's home at 17 with no money. He built his company from the ground up over three decades.[1]

characters.name: Arthur Frobisher
characters.description: Arthur Frobisheris a main character in thefirst,second, andthirdseasons ofDamages. Frobisher's great-great-grandfather designed guns and became wealthy during the Civil War due to munitions demands. The Depression wiped out their wealth, except for his gun collection. He founded a private club, which remained standing over 100 years later and contained a display of his gun collection. Frobisher grew up with nothing and left his father's home at 17 with no money. He built his company from the ground up over three decades.[1]

characters.name: Carol Tobin
characters.description: Carol Tobinis a recurring character in thethird seasonofDamages. Carol Tobin was loyal to her father,Louis Tobin. Manipulated by her brother,Joe Tobin, she killedDanielle Marchetti.

characters.name: Carrie Parsons
characters.description: Carrie Parsonsis a minor character in thefirst seasonand a recurring character in thethird seasonsofDamages. Carrie was born three years before her sister,Ellen Parsons,[1]and had another sister,Megan Parsons. She came from a working-class background and lived in a home with an abusive father,Gary Parsons. Her mother wasDeniece Parsons. Carrie implied that she was not a good role model for Ellen.[1]

characters.name: Catherine Hewes
characters.description: Catherine Hewesis a recurring character in thefourthandfifthseasons ofDamages. Catherine is a young girl, turning four years old in Season 5.[1]She is soft-spoken and does not speak much. She is the daughter ofMichael HewesandJill Burnham.Patty Hewesis her paternal grandmother.

characters.name: Channing McClaren
characters.description: Channing McClarenis a recurring character in thefifth seasonofDamages. McClaren was the founder ofMcClarenTruth, a website that published information provided by whistleblowers. He sought legal guidance fromPatty Hewes, who recommendedEllen Parsonsinstead. AfterNaomi Wallingprovided him with concerning information about her employer,Princefield Investments, the leak exposed her own personal information as well. After Naomi's death, Patty filed a wrongful death suit against McClaren, representing Naomi's daughter,Rachel Walling.

characters.name: Channing McClaren
characters.description: Channing McClarenis a recurring character in thefifth seasonofDamages. McClaren was the founder ofMcClarenTruth, a website that published information provided by whistleblowers. He sought legal guidance fromPatty Hewes, who recommendedEllen Parsonsinstead. AfterNaomi Wallingprovided him with concerning information about her employer,Princefield Investments, the leak exposed her own personal information as well. After Naomi's death, Patty filed a wrongful death suit against McClaren, representing Naomi's daughter,Rachel Walling.

characters.name: Chris Sanchez
characters.description: Chris Sanchezis a recurring character in thefourthandfifthseasons ofDamages. Chris was a decorated soldier in the US Military. After finishing his contract with the military, he signed up to be a private contractor withHigh Star Security Corporation. He returned home in October of 2010 with PTSD after all of his men were killed on an unsanctioned mission in Afghanistan. Chris was sought out by his former high school friendEllen Parsons, who wanted to use him for information and as a witness in her case againstHigh Star Security Corporationand its CEO,Howard Erickson. Shortly after he gave information to her, he was persuaded into returning to Afghanistan to work for High Star again. While over there, Ellen continued to pursue her case against the company. When the judge required a personal statement from Chris, they held a friend of his at gun point while he perjured himself on Skype, which was unknown to everyone back home. They then locked him up for months with the intent to kill him. Ellen agreed to settle her case against High Star in exchange for his safe return when she found out from a reporter stationed in Afghanistan that his murder had been set up. Right before the final exchange,Patty Hewesruined it by creating another case against the company, but Chris managed to escape.

characters.name: Chris Sanchez
characters.description: Chris Sanchezis a recurring character in thefourthandfifthseasons ofDamages. Chris was a decorated soldier in the US Military. After finishing his contract with the military, he signed up to be a private contractor withHigh Star Security Corporation. He returned home in October of 2010 with PTSD after all of his men were killed on an unsanctioned mission in Afghanistan. Right before the final exchange,Patty Hewesruined it by creating another case against the company, but Chris managed to escape.

characters.name: Claire Maddox
characters.description: Claire Maddoxis a recurring character in thesecond seasonofDamages. Claire Maddox was the in-house counsel atUltima National Resourcesand chief adviser to its CEO,Walter Kendrick. She thrived on confrontation, including with attorneyPatty Hewes, who brought a legal case against Kendrick and UNR. Claire had an affair withDaniel Purcellwhile he was married. Her devotion to Kendrick was challenged the deeper she was drawn into his conspiracy. She saw an opportunity to oust Kendrick when she discovered he had been lying to her, but he used her history of sexual indiscretions to have her thrown out of the company before she could oust him.

characters.name: Claire Maddox
characters.description: Claire Maddoxis a recurring character in thesecond seasonofDamages. Claire Maddox was the in-house counsel atUltima National Resourcesand chief adviser to its CEO,Walter Kendrick. She thrived on confrontation, including with attorneyPatty Hewes, who brought a legal case against Kendrick and UNR. Claire had an affair withDaniel Purcellwhile he was married. Her devotion to Kendrick was challenged the deeper she was drawn into his conspiracy. She saw an opportunity to oust Kendrick when she discovered he had been lying to her, but he used her history of sexual indiscretions to have her thrown out of the company before she could oust him.

characters.name: Dan Williams
characters.description: Dan Williamsis a minor character in thefirstandfifthseasons ofDamages. Williams was a detective who worked closely with DetectiveRosario Ortizto solve the murder ofDavid Connorand, years later, the missing persons case ofEllen Parsons.

characters.name: Daniel Purcell
characters.description: Daniel Purcellis a recurring character in thesecond seasonofDamages. Daniel was a brilliant scientist. He was diabetic and had a temper. His past relationship withPatty Hewesresulted in a son,Michael Hewes, whom he did not raise. He also had a daughter,Erica Purcell. He cheated on his wife,Christine Purcell, withUltima National ResourcescounselClaire Maddox.

characters.name: Daniel Purcell
characters.description: Daniel Purcellis a recurring character in thesecond seasonofDamages. Daniel was a brilliant scientist. He was diabetic and had a temper. His past relationship withPatty Hewesresulted in a son,Michael Hewes, whom he did not raise. He also had a daughter,Erica Purcell. He cheated on his wife,Christine Purcell, withUltima National ResourcescounselClaire Maddox.

characters.name: Danielle Marchetti
characters.description: Danielle Marchettiis a minor character in thethird seasonofDamages. Danielle had a daughter as a result of an affair with a married man,Joe Tobin. No father was listed on the child's birth certificate. Joe's father,Louis Tobin, allowed everyone to believe he had had the affair and that Tessa was his daughter in order to protect Joe amid his fragile mental state. Louis also financially supported them. Danielle was killed byCarol Tobin, who had been manipulated by her brother,Joe Tobin. Her death became part of the fraud case brought byPatty Hewesagainst the Tobin family.

characters.name: Dave Pell
characters.description: Dave Pellis a recurring character in thesecond seasonofDamages. Dave Pell was a power broker involved withWalter Kendrickand the corrupt FBI investigation againstPatty Hewes. He made money manipulating the energy market. He attempted to get Patty to drop her case againstUltima National Resourcesby going to her husband and an old friend of his,Phil Grey. Pell informed Phil of an FBI investigation against Patty and later told Phil that stock in UNR was at an all time low and about to go up. Both times Phil urged Patty to drop the case, but she did not. To create further conflict, Pell also nominated Phil for Energy Secretary. Pell ended up getting arrested for his crimes.

characters.name: David Connor
characters.description: David Connoris a main character in thefirstseason, a recurring character in thesecond,third, andfifthseasons, and makes an uncredited appearance in thefourthseason ofDamages. David was a first-year surgical intern and had a girlfriend,Ellen Parsons. They became engaged after she found the engagement ring he had hidden in a small bag he had placed among her shopping bags. David was confident that their relationship would survive the demands their careers placed on them. However, he was not happy and feared for her safety when he discovered that his sister,Katie Connor, may have had information relevant to the case againstArthur Frobisherthat Ellen was pursuing at work after recently being hired atHewes & Associates. They called off their engagement due to Ellen's refusal to stand up to the demands of her boss,Patty Hewes, and her prioritization of theFrobisher caseover their relationship. David was murdered by Arthur Frobisher's hitman,Rick Messer.

characters.name: David Connor
characters.description: David Connoris a main character in thefirstseason, a recurring character in thesecond,third, andfifthseasons, and makes an uncredited appearance in thefourthseason ofDamages. David was a first-year surgical intern and had a girlfriend,Ellen Parsons. They became engaged after she found the engagement ring he had hidden in a small bag he had placed among her shopping bags. David was confident that their relationship would survive the demands their careers placed on them. However, he was not happy and feared for her safety when he discovered that his sister,Katie Connor, may have had information relevant to the case againstArthur Frobisherthat Ellen was pursuing at work after recently being hired atHewes & Associates. They called off their engagement due to Ellen's refusal to stand up to the demands of her boss,Patty Hewes, and her prioritization of theFrobisher caseover their relationship. David was murdered by Arthur Frobisher's hitman,Rick Messer.

characters.name: Deniece Parsons
characters.description: Deniece Parsonsis a minor character in all five seasons ofDamages. Deniece had three daughters:Carrie Parsons, followed three years later byEllen Parsons,[1]and another daughter,Megan Parsons. She was married toGary Parsons, who was abusive. They were working-class. She had two granddaughters. Carrie had a daughter namedCharlotte, and Ellen had a daughter.

characters.name: Ellen Parsons
characters.description: Ellen Parsonsis a main character in all five seasons ofDamages. Ellen is a bright and idealistic law school graduate who lands a job at the prestigious law firmHewes & Associates. While working forPatty Hewesin a win-at-all-costs environment, Ellen evolves into a clever, manipulative, and vengeful lawyer. However, her own sense of ethics leads her to simultaneously work as an informant for the FBI. She later joins the public attorney's office, followed by a brief return to renowned law firmNye, Everett & Polk, and finally as the head of her own law firm,Parsons & Associates. Ellen was born three years after her older sister,Carrie Parsons, and had another sister,Megan Parsons. She came from a working-class background and lived in a home with an abusive father,Gary Parsons. Her mother wasDeniece Parsons.

characters.name: Ellen Parsons
characters.description: Ellen Parsonsis a main character in all five seasons ofDamages. Ellen is a bright and idealistic law school graduate who lands a job at the prestigious law firmHewes & Associates. While working forPatty Hewesin a win-at-all-costs environment, Ellen evolves into a clever, manipulative, and vengeful lawyer. However, her own sense of ethics leads her to simultaneously work as an informant for the FBI. She later joins the public attorney's office, followed by a brief return to renowned law firmNye, Everett & Polk, and finally as the head of her own law firm,Parsons & Associates. Ellen was born three years after her older sister,Carrie Parsons, and had another sister,Megan Parsons. She came from a working-class background and lived in a home with an abusive father,Gary Parsons. Her mother wasDeniece Parsons.

characters.name: Felicia Marquand
characters.description: Felicia Marquandis a recurring character in thefirst seasonofDamages. Felicia Marquand is an employee atHewes & Associates. Outside the courthouse, Felicia walked down the stairs asPatty HewesandMartin Cutler, attorneys on opposing sides of a case, walk up them. Felicia let Patty know that the verdict was in, which, along with further prodding from Patty, caused Cutler to agree to settle the case at the price Patty wanted for her clients. After Cutler called to inform the judge, Patty greeted a woman who was returning from lunch as Madame Foreman. Cutler became angry, realizing he had been tricked.

characters.name: Finn Garrity
characters.description: Finn Garrityis a minor character in thesecond seasonofDamages. Finn Garrity was a Wall Street energy futures trader. He was involved in a market manipulation scheme withUltima National ResourcesCEOWalter Kendrickand power brokerDave Pell. Kendrick's company limited energy supply to create power outages, creating artificial demand for energy and causing energy prices to rise. He informed Garrity of when his company would do this, allowing them to time the markets and make large profits. Patty Heweslearned of the scheme and brought a case against UNR and Kendrick. At first refusing to help Patty with the case, he later changed his mind and plead with her to allow him to testify. When she refused, having already found another path forward, he stabbed her. Her wounds were not fatal.

characters.name: Gary Parsons
characters.description: Gary Parsonsis a minor character in thefirst,third, andfifthseasons ofDamages. Gary had three daughters:Carrie Parsons, followed three years later byEllen Parsons,[1]and another daughter,Megan Parsons. He was married toDeniece Parsons. They were working-class. He was abusive. She had two granddaughters. Carrie had a daughter namedCharlotte, and Ellen had a daughter.

characters.name: George Moore
characters.description: George Mooreis a recurring character in thefirst seasonofDamages. George Moore worked for the SEC and was involved in hindering the SEC's investigation againstArthur Frobisher. At Moore's direction,Gregory Malinawas hit by a car and killed.

characters.name: Gregory Malina
characters.description: Gregory Malinais a recurring character in thefirst seasonofDamages. Gregory Malina was a consultant with critical information for the casePatty Heweswas bringing againstArthur Frobisher. He had had a one-night stand withKatie Connorin Florida, which resulted in a pregnancy that he accompanied her to terminate. Afterwards, they lost contact due to Katie's own admission that she had been really messed up at the time. Katie claimed to Patty that she had not seen him for five years, but they reconnected in New York City and began a relationship. He proceeded to feed her false information about himself, such as that he was now married, and about the night they were together. He told Katie a false identity of the person they had seenArthur Frobisherwith in Florida in order to avoid having to testify himself.

characters.name: Gregory Malina
characters.description: Gregory Malinais a recurring character in thefirst seasonofDamages. Gregory Malina was a consultant with critical information for the casePatty Heweswas bringing againstArthur Frobisher. He had had a one-night stand withKatie Connorin Florida, which resulted in a pregnancy that he accompanied her to terminate. Afterwards, they lost contact due to Katie's own admission that she had been really messed up at the time. Katie claimed to Patty that she had not seen him for five years, but they reconnected in New York City and began a relationship. He proceeded to feed her false information about himself, such as that he was now married, and about the night they were together. He told Katie a false identity of the person they had seenArthur Frobisherwith in Florida in order to avoid having to testify himself.

characters.name: Hollis Nye
characters.description: Hollis Nyeis a recurring character in thefirstandsecondseasons ofDamages. Hollis Nye was an attorney and senior partner atNye, Everett & Polk. He had beenEllen Parsons'mentor while she was a summer associate at his firm after law school. His firm offered her a job as a junior associate, but upon learning that she had an interview atHewes & Associates, he all but considered her chances of choosing his firm to be nil. He caught up with Ellen at abarand warned her aboutPatty Hewes, having her sign and keep hisbusiness cardas proof of this warning and to clear his conscience.[2]

characters.name: Howard Erickson
characters.description: Howard Ericksonis a recurring character in thefourth seasonofDamages. Founder and CEO ofHigh Star Security Corporation, Erickson made a fortune supplying the US Government with security forces in Afghanistan and was protected by his connections to the highest echelons of power in Washington, DC. Fiercely loyal to his country, he proved to be a formidable opponent forPatty HewesandEllen Parsons. Erickson was a religious man, and he delivered a sermon on Christmas Eve at the urging of his pastor.

characters.name: Howard Erickson
characters.description: Howard Ericksonis a recurring character in thefourth seasonofDamages. Founder and CEO ofHigh Star Security Corporation, Erickson made a fortune supplying the US Government with security forces in Afghanistan and was protected by his connections to the highest echelons of power in Washington, DC. Fiercely loyal to his country, he proved to be a formidable opponent forPatty HewesandEllen Parsons. Erickson was a religious man, and he delivered a sermon on Christmas Eve at the urging of his pastor.

characters.name: Jerry Boorman
characters.description: Jerry Boormanis a recurring character in thefourth seasonofDamages. Boorman was a shadowy opportunist with ties to bothHoward Ericksonand the Middle East. He was intimately involved in the conspiracy at the heart of the lawsuit againstHigh Star Security Corporation.

characters.name: Jerry Boorman
characters.description: Jerry Boormanis a recurring character in thefourth seasonofDamages. Boorman was a shadowy opportunist with ties to bothHoward Ericksonand the Middle East. He was intimately involved in the conspiracy at the heart of the lawsuit againstHigh Star Security Corporation.

characters.name: Jill Burnham
characters.description: Jill Burnhamis a recurring character in thesecondandthirdseasons ofDamages. Directly after earning her PhD degree, Jill taught art history at Columbia University from 1988 to 1992. She owned an art gallery in Chelsea, New York.

characters.name: Joe Tobin
characters.description: Joe Tobinis a recurring character in thethird seasonofDamages. Joe's world fell apart when his father,Louis Tobin, confessed that their family's investment business was a complete fraud. He must decide whether to lead his family forward by cooperating withPatty Hewes'investigation or circle the wagons and fight her attempts to destroy his family's future. Joe Tobin killedTom Shayes.

characters.name: Joe Tobin
characters.description: Joe Tobinis a recurring character in thethird seasonofDamages. Joe's world fell apart when his father,Louis Tobin, confessed that their family's investment business was a complete fraud. He must decide whether to lead his family forward by cooperating withPatty Hewes'investigation or circle the wagons and fight her attempts to destroy his family's future. Joe Tobin killedTom Shayes.

characters.name: Josh Reston
characters.description: Josh Restonis a minor character in thesecondandthirdseasons ofDamages. Josh Reston was a journalist investigating pollution in West Virginia byUltima National Resources. He had a brief relationship withEllen Parsons. He also gave her information aboutLeonard Winstone, which she passed on toPatty Hewesin her case against the Tobins.

characters.name: Kate Franklin
characters.description: Kate Franklinis a recurring character onSeason 5ofDamages. A former colleague ofPatty Hewes, Kate Franklin aims to take on Patty by working forEllen Parsons. Her knowledge of Patty's practice makes her particularly valuable to Ellen. She is also Patty's sister on their father's side,[1]therefore passionate about having Patty and their father make amends, not only as a lawyer, but also as a family member. Kate's father wasLyle Hewes. Sharing a father, she was the half-sister ofPatty Hewes.

characters.name: Kate Franklin
characters.description: Kate Franklinis a recurring character onSeason 5ofDamages. A former colleague ofPatty Hewes, Kate Franklin aims to take on Patty by working forEllen Parsons. Her knowledge of Patty's practice makes her particularly valuable to Ellen. She is also Patty's sister on their father's side,[1]therefore passionate about having Patty and their father make amends, not only as a lawyer, but also as a family member. Kate's father wasLyle Hewes. Sharing a father, she was the half-sister ofPatty Hewes.

characters.name: Katie Connor
characters.description: Katie Connoris a recurring character in thefirst seasonand a main character in thesecond seasonofDamages. Katie was a chef. She trained at the Culinary Institute.[1] She had a history of drug abuse.

characters.name: Katie Connor
characters.description: Katie Connoris a recurring character in thefirst seasonand a main character in thesecond seasonofDamages. Katie was a chef. She trained at the Culinary Institute.[1] She had a history of drug abuse.

characters.name: L.J. Werner
characters.description: L.J. Werneris a minor character in thefirst seasonand a recurring character in thesecond seasonofDamages. L.J. Werner was an agent in the FBI who worked closely with AgentRandall Harrison. They approachedEllen Parsonsand convinced her to become an informant in their case againstPatty Hewes. Werner accepted money fromDave Pellto keep him updated on the FBI's case against Patty. He often received phone calls from Pell and excused himself to speak alone, telling Harrison it was his ex-wife,Pam, calling about their divorce settlement. When Werner's cell phone rang while he was out of the room and its display indicated that the call was from Pam, Harrison answered it and told her to back off, but a male voice was on the other end.

characters.name: Larry Popler
characters.description: Larry Popleris a recurring character in thefirst seasonofDamages. Larry had served as a Marine in the US Military. He was married toMildred Popler. Larry Popler was a former Custodial Services employee of 34 years atArthur Frobisher'scompany, having lost his job and savings after Frobisher told his employees to invest in the company before dumping his own stock. He was one of 5,000 former employees involved in a lawsuit against Frobisher brought byPatty HewesofHewes & Associates.

characters.name: Leonard Winstone
characters.description: Leonard Winstoneis a recurring character in thethird seasonofDamages. Leonard Winstone was born Lester Wiggins to motherBarbara Wigginsand fatherAlbert Wiggins. His father was a con man. Lester had been a small time criminal before dropping off the radar in the 1980s, when he stole the identity of the real Leonard Winstone, who had died in a car accident.

characters.name: Leonard Winstone
characters.description: Leonard Winstoneis a recurring character in thethird seasonofDamages. Leonard Winstone was born Lester Wiggins to motherBarbara Wigginsand fatherAlbert Wiggins. His father was a con man. Lester had been a small time criminal before dropping off the radar in the 1980s, when he stole the identity of the real Leonard Winstone, who had died in a car accident.

characters.name: Lila DiMeo
characters.description: Lila DiMeois a recurring character in thefirst seasonofDamages. Lila took a liking toDavid Connorwhile he cared for her grandfather, a patient of his. He rejected her. She stalked and harassed him and his fiance,Ellen Parsons, stealing a key to their apartment and sneaking inside. She lied to Ellen and the police about the nature of her and David's relationship, saying they were together.

characters.name: Louis Tobin
characters.description: Louis Tobinis a recurring character in thethird seasonofDamages. Louis Tobin committed the largest investment fraud in US history, having run his investment business as a Ponzi scheme to cover his sonJoe Tobin'sover promising to investors. He confessed the fraud to his family, who claimed to not have known. He had stashed money away for his family, which Joe searched for desperately. Patty HewesandTom Shayeswere tasked by the US government to recover the stolen money for defrauded investors.

characters.name: Lyle Hewes
characters.description: Lyle Hewesis a minor character in thefifth seasonofDamages. Lyle had two daughters,Patty HewesandKate Franklin, who had different mothers. He was abusive toward Patty and her mother. He had an alcohol addiction. When Lyle became seriously ill, Kate, acting as his lawyer, contacted Patty in order to get her to sign off on her part of his land. In her capacity as family, Kate also urged Patty to contact him to make amends. Patty resisted both pleas at first, but eventually showed up at his bedside in the hospital. They had not had contact in many years. Patty began with speaking about forgiveness. He immediately changed the subject to his land, which she then promised to sell to the lowest bidder. He said he had made her tough and that she was what she was because of him. She told him that she was who she was despite him. When he apologized, she did not accept it.

characters.name: Marilyn Tobin
characters.description: Marilyn Tobinis a recurring character in thethird seasonofDamages. When her husband,Louis Tobin, confessed that his investment business was a complete fraud, Marilyn Tobin tried to hold her family together in the aftermath. As she prepared for her husband's incarceration, Marilyn, always up for a good fight, confronted the reality that the opulent lifestyle she had enjoyed for decades was over and thatPatty Heweswas out to destroy her. In the end, Marilyn's scheming not only failed to save the family's fortune, but destroyed the family as well. Disowned by her sonJoe Tobin, Marilyn realized that her world had been destroyed. She committed suicide by jumping off a bridge into the East River.

characters.name: Marilyn Tobin
characters.description: Marilyn Tobinis a recurring character in thethird seasonofDamages. When her husband,Louis Tobin, confessed that his investment business was a complete fraud, Marilyn Tobin tried to hold her family together in the aftermath. As she prepared for her husband's incarceration, Marilyn, always up for a good fight, confronted the reality that the opulent lifestyle she had enjoyed for decades was over and thatPatty Heweswas out to destroy her. In the end, Marilyn's scheming not only failed to save the family's fortune, but destroyed the family as well. Disowned by her sonJoe Tobin, Marilyn realized that her world had been destroyed. She committed suicide by jumping off a bridge into the East River.
